title: Verify + vendor one open Latin font through the gate
context: All glyph outlines must come from an OFL, PD/CC0, or CC font — no proprietary school or handwriting font. The project's primary Latin candidate is SIL Andika (OFL), a literacy-oriented font, but its fitness as a formation model font (not just a reading font) must be verified, its license snapshot committed, and it must pass the provenance gate defined in gate-002. Until one font is gate-verified and vendored, no Latin glyph can be modeled or rendered.
objective: Identify, evaluate, and vendor at least one openly-licensed Latin font through the gate-002 provenance gate: confirm OFL (or equivalent) terms permit redistribution and derivatives, verify fitness for formation modeling, commit a license snapshot (local copy + SHA-256 + Wayback URL), and produce a gate PASS artifact.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one Latin font (primary candidate: SIL Andika OFL) is confirmed to permit redistribution and derivatives under OFL or equivalent open terms.
Font fitness for formation modeling is documented: glyph outlines are clear enough for tracing guides; literacy design does not impede formation clarity.
License snapshot committed: local copy of OFL text, SHA-256 hash, and Wayback Machine URL, following the gate-002 snapshot format.
OFL Reserved Font Name status checked and recorded; overlay approach confirmed to avoid RFN violations.
gate-002 PASS artifact issued for this font and committed to the repo.
If SIL Andika is found unfit, at least one alternate open Latin font is evaluated and the decision documented.
resources: gate-002 policy document and snapshot format
output: A committed provenance record for the chosen Latin font (license snapshot + SHA-256 + Wayback URL + gate PASS artifact), plus a brief evaluation note on formation fitness, vendored into data/latin/ or a designated fonts/ directory.
